html:
Exclusive to:
This content is available only after activating the Lunar Rift

“"Well, A Rather Beautifully Intelligent System." Uses a complicated assortment of cogs and circuitry for computing.
Once a target has been selected, as long as it stays in range, the W.A.R.B.I.S. begins calculating optimal performance to fight the target.
The head gear improves the wearer's damage as it analyzes the target, ending at a 20% damage increase.
When combined with the W.A.R.B.I.S. armor, the two will sync up for a few moments sharing target information.
Wearing the W.A.R.B.I.S. Head Gear also provides the wearer with a Dramatically Updating Diagram (or D.U.D.) which provides information on which target is currently being analyzed and what level the W.A.R.B.I.S. has reached.”

-

The W.A.R.B.I.S. Head Gear is a craftable Armor exclusive to Don't Starve Together. It requires 8 Scrap, 3 Electrical Doodads, and 1 Enlightened Shard to craft after learning the rare Blueprint that has a chance to drop from hammering Abandoned Junk.

The W.A.R.B.I.S. Head Gear has a Durability of 730 (over twice as durable as a Football Helmet), and absorbs 85% of all Physical Damage and a flat 5 Planar Damage. It also insulates the player against Lightning strikes, turns all Electric Damage into Physical Damage, and ignore Shock effect.

Wearing and fighting with it analyzes a single target and increases the Physical Damage done by the wearer over time up to a maximum of +20% damage after a period of 20 seconds. This bonus is reset after either attacking a different target, or walking too far away (4 Tiles) from the current target. An overlay called the Dramatically Updating Diagram (D.U.D.) appears over the target, showing the analysis progress.

Analysis
Stage Time to reach Damage Boost
0 Immediate +5%
1 10 seconds +10%
2 16 seconds +15%
3 20 seconds +20%

The W.A.R.B.I.S. Head Gear can be combined with the W.A.R.B.I.S. Armor to get a speed boost in addition to the damage. The Armor will get synced with the current stage of the Head Gear within a few seconds, shown through waves at the top of the diagram, so the speed boost can be obtained without an additional analysis. When taking off the Head Gear or the Armor, the current state of the Analysis remains as long as the other is worn.

When the W.A.R.B.I.S. Head Gear's durability reaches 0%, it does not disappear; instead, it is unequipped and can not be equipped until repaired. It can be fully repaired by one Auto-Mat-O-Chanic.

Contents

  • 1 Skill Tree (WX-78)
    • 1.1 Beta Circuits Tinkering I
  • 2 Quotes
  • 3 Tips
  • 4 Trivia
  • 5 Gallery

Skill Tree (WX-78)

Beta Circuits Tinkering I

WX-78 with "Beta Circuits Tinkering I" skill and Rangebooster Circuits can increase the range of W.A.R.B.I.S target locking. Each increase 6 Wall units (1.5 Tiles).

Quotes

Locking on to target

  • W.A.R.B.I.S. HAS NEW TARGET!
  • W.A.R.B.I.S. IS ANALYZING...
  • W.A.R.B.I.S. UPGRADING!
  • W.A.R.B.I.S. AMPLIFYING!!
  • W.A.R.B.I.S. EXTREME FUNCTION!!!

Syncing up with Armor

  • W.A.R.B.I.S. IS SYNCHING...
  • W.A.R.B.I.S. HAS SYNCHED.

Losing target from being too far or attacking different target

  • W.A.R.B.I.S. TRACKING LOST.

Tips

  • Due to gaining damage over time when fighting a single target, the helmet is extremely useful against Boss Monsters, especially those that do not summon minions.

Trivia

  • W.A.R.B.I.S. Head Gear was introduced in the Host of Horrors update.
  • The name of the item may be a play on words, as a literal "gear on the head" (normally, the word is written as "headgear").

Gallery

  • Dropped W.A.R.B.I.S. Head Gear
  • Dropped Broken W.A.R.B.I.S. Head Gear
  • Lock-on HUD element
  • W.A.R.B.I.S. Head Gear concept animation from Rhymes with Play
Tools and WeaponsCraftable Items

DSTDSRoGSWHam

Broken
"Look smart, fight smart."
Object Info

Increases player's damage over time while locked on to an enemy.

Repaired By:

"wagpunkhat"
Exclusive to:
This content is available only after activating the Lunar Rift
“It really gets my gears turning.”
–Wilson
“Aww, it's a hat that teaches nerds how to fight!”
–Willow
“Robot hat make Wolfgang more deadly? Impossible. Give to me.”
–Wolfgang
“How freeing it would be, to think like an emotionless machine.”
–Wendy
“A GOOD HAT. WE WILL DO UNSPEAKABLY VIOLENT THINGS TOGETHER”
–WX-78
“It analyzes combat in order to augment the physical capabilities of the wearer.”
–Wickerbottom
“Looks like some sorta techie tin toque.”
–Woodie
“This machine really thinks it can tell me what to do?”
–Maxwell
“This helm döth be pössessed by the spirit öf a cunning warriör.”
–Wigfrid
“What's one more voice in our head?”
–Webber
“It stirs up such pugnacious thoughts in my mind!”
–Warly
“Just like robot friend!”
–Wormwood
“Love me a steam-powered murder helmet as much as the next gal but clunky much?”
–Winona
“This should help me blow off some steam, hyuyu!”
–Wortox
“Nice Ironfolk hat.”
–Wurt
“It's going to suck out my brain! Ha ha, probably not.”
–Walter
“I'd love to take it apart and see how it ticks.”
–Wanda
